def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    import re

    if not triples:
        return ""

    def clean(text: str) -> str:
        s = str(text).strip()
        if len(s) >= 2 and s[0] == '"' and s[-1] == '"':
            return s[1:-1]
        return s

    def join_parts(parts: list[str]) -> str:
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        if len(parts) == 2:
            return f"{parts[0]} and {parts[1]}"
        return f"{', '.join(parts[:-1])}, and {parts[-1]}"

    def render(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        o = clean(obj)

        direct = {
            "cityServed": f"serves {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"is {o} metres above sea level",
            "location": f"is located in {o}",
            "operatingOrganisation": f"is operated by {o}",
            "runwayLength": f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
            "runwayName": f"has runway named {o}",
            "country": f"is in {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"is part of {o}",
            "icaoLocationIdentifier": f"has ICAO location identifier {o}",
            "locationIdentifier": f"has location identifier {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": f"is {o} feet above sea level",
            "iataLocationIdentifier": f"has IATA location identifier {o}",
            "nativeName": f"is also known as {o}",
            "leaderParty": f"has leader party {o}",
            "capital": f"has capital {o}",
            "language": f"uses {o}",
            "leader": f"is led by {o}",
            "owner": f"is owned by {o}",
            "largestCity": f"has largest city {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": f"is {o} metres above sea level",
            "administrativeArrondissement": f"is in the arrondissement of {o}",
            "mayor": f"has mayor {o}",
            "runwaySurfaceType": f"has runway surface type {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"has official language {o}",
            "city": f"is in the city of {o}",
            "jurisdiction": f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
            "demonym": f"has demonym {o}",
            "aircraftHelicopter": f"uses helicopter {o}",
            "transportAircraft": f"uses transport aircraft {o}",
            "currency": f"uses currency {o}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered at {o}",
            "class": f"belongs to class {o}",
            "division": f"belongs to division {o}",
            "order": f"belongs to order {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"has leader title {o}",
            "hubAirport": f"has hub airport {o}",
            "aircraftFighter": f"uses fighter aircraft {o}",
            "attackAircraft": f"uses attack aircraft {o}",
            "battle": f"fought in {o}",
            "countySeat": f"has county seat {o}",
            "chief": f"has chief {o}",
            "foundedBy": f"was founded by {o}",
            "postalCode": f"has postal code {o}",
            "areaCode": f"has area code {o}",
            "foundingYear": f"was founded in {o}",
            "ceremonialCounty": f"is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
        }
        if pred in direct:
            return direct[pred]

        m = re.fullmatch(r"(\d+(?:st|nd|rd|th))Runway(LengthFeet|LengthMetre|Number|SurfaceType)", pred)
        if m:
            n, kind = m.groups()
            if kind == "LengthFeet":
                return f"has {n} runway length of {o} feet"
            if kind == "LengthMetre":
                return f"has {n} runway length of {o} metres"
            if kind == "Number":
                return f"has {n} runway number {o}"
            if kind == "SurfaceType":
                return f"has {n} runway surface type {o}"

        label = re.sub(r"([a-z])([A-Z])", r"\1 \2", pred).lower()
        return f"has {label} {o}"

    grouped: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    clauses = []
    for subject, facts in grouped.items():
        fragments = [render(p, o) for p, o in facts]
        clauses.append(f"{subject} {join_parts(fragments)}")

    return f"{'; '.join(clauses)}."
